title: Fastest Music Distribution Service
metaTitle: Fastest Music Distribution Service | Distrozi Music Distribution
metaDescription: Read about Fastest Music Distribution Service. Learn how to distribute your music globally, protect copyrights, and maximize streaming royalties with Distrozi.
author: Distrozi Editorial Team

content.text: Navigating the digital music ecosystem requires a clear understanding of how distributors, stores, and registration agencies interact. Finding the best music distribution service is crucial to get your music on Spotify, Apple Music, and TikTok while keeping your master rights.
Below, we outline the leading platforms, structuring their workflow models, pros, cons, and explaining which types of artists or labels they fit best.

Distrozi is positioned for artists, managers, and labels that want distribution, catalog control, YouTube CMS support, and release operations handled in one place. It is a strong fit when the priority is clean metadata, reliable delivery, and long-term control of a growing catalog rather than only uploading a single release. For speed-focused releases, fast delivery only helps when metadata, artwork, artist profiles, and review timelines are prepared early enough to avoid delays or post-release corrections.

DistroKid is a self-service distributor known for a fast upload flow and broad DSP delivery. It can work well for artists who release often, but users should still plan metadata, profile setup, release dates, and post-release updates carefully so speed does not create avoidable catalog issues. For speed-focused releases, fast delivery only helps when metadata, artwork, artist profiles, and review timelines are prepared early enough to avoid delays or post-release corrections.

TuneCore is an established distribution platform with store delivery, analytics, creator tools, and publishing-related options in its wider ecosystem. It is usually considered by artists who want a familiar dashboard and deeper reporting while keeping release administration organized across multiple platforms. For speed-focused releases, fast delivery only helps when metadata, artwork, artist profiles, and review timelines are prepared early enough to avoid delays or post-release corrections.

CD Baby is a long-running aggregator with a structured release workflow and catalog-oriented services. It is often considered by artists who want a more guided distribution path, useful documentation, and support for releases that may need careful setup beyond a quick upload. For speed-focused releases, fast delivery only helps when metadata, artwork, artist profiles, and review timelines are prepared early enough to avoid delays or post-release corrections.

LANDR combines distribution with creator tools such as mastering, production resources, collaboration features, and release preparation support. It is most relevant for artists who want parts of the recording, polishing, and delivery process connected in one creative workflow. For speed-focused releases, fast delivery only helps when metadata, artwork, artist profiles, and review timelines are prepared early enough to avoid delays or post-release corrections.
Speed-focused distribution still needs careful metadata, review planning, and a reliable path for corrections when a release is close.

platformItems.name: Distrozi
platformItems.description: Distrozi is built for artists and labels that need global DSP delivery, YouTube CMS support, catalog management, and release operations from one dashboard.

platformItems.name: DistroKid
platformItems.description: DistroKid is a familiar self-service distributor for artists who want quick upload workflows and wide DSP availability.

platformItems.name: TuneCore
platformItems.description: TuneCore suits artists who want established store delivery, creator tools, and publishing-related options in a self-service workflow.

platformItems.name: RouteNote
platformItems.description: RouteNote gives independent artists a straightforward route into major DSPs with flexible account paths and simple catalog controls.

platformItems.name: Amuse
platformItems.description: Amuse is designed around a lightweight artist workflow, making it useful for creators who prefer simple release management.

platformItems.name: ONErpm
platformItems.description: ONErpm is a strong fit for artists and labels that want global delivery combined with regional teams and marketing-oriented support.

faq.question: How long does it take for a song to go live on Spotify?
faq.answer: Delivery takes 24-48 hours. However, we suggest scheduling your release at least 14 days in advance to allow for playlist pitching.

faq.question: What is the difference between an ISRC code and a UPC code?
faq.answer: An ISRC code tracks streams and play metrics for individual songs, while a UPC code tracks sales metrics for the overall single or album release product.
